reviews.review_description: I bought this game not knowing how it would be but it’s a fun game to pass the time by yourself or with family. It would be awesome if the developers would add more games and just keep adding more activities. I think this game should continue growing.
I was expecting the game to be more challenging. I feel like it's too easy as if it hands out participation points as opposed to points based on skill.
My kid loves it! It's easy to learn to use the controls and it's pretty fun for adults too! Def worth the price
So sad about this, it’s a beautiful world. Grabbing magic wand is impossible. Refund please and thank you

You are in like a carnival and can pick different games to play- hoops,mini golf-fire hose game-magic wand game and table turning game -earn tickets to win objects to use in games like a skull basketball for hoops game 
Can play with multiple players taking turns also!!! Family loved this game and the price is awesome!!!!!
What a value!!!!
Very disappointed

developer_response.body: We're really sorry to hear that! If you want to share any details on why you are disappointed with the game, please know that our inbox is always open: support@resolutiongames.com.

reviews.review_description:
I had this on the Go and when I saw this for quest, had to have it. However this is a disappointing rewrite. Much of it is identical but where they have seen fit to make use of 6dof have messed up the best of the games.

The wand waving game is the best conversion and uses 6dof perfectly. The only improvement.

Firefighting has a balcony getting in the way, so this was a nice sit down game, now you have to stand to see over the balcony. No.

Cow table racing. Nothing changed it works. Not favourite though.

Golf, what is going on with the control system, I think this could be better needs more practice.

Hammer the plates. Thisbis just plain broken, it doesn't register I banged the gong. Over and over and over I hit it to start the game and nothing, occasionally registers on a back swing thru the gong. Then the game is pretty bad anyway seems that I have to hit everything 10 times more than on the go version.

Lastly the best game is ruined. Hamster basketball. First that commentating hamster is in the wrong place its been repositioned in front of the targets, move the thing. The use of 6dof for aim and throw and fire is terrible, this was a nice flick of the wrist moderately tricky, now this thing wants echo vr throwing skills and the constant pickup instead if being there to throw is ruining the gameplay. 

The devs need to go back to what made this game great on the go, remember it's for kids and a pick up and drop mini game system. Instead of just converting what you had, you thought you would tweak it and you forgot the basics of what makes a good game. 

If they fix this I'll change this review, don't waste you money on a this till the devs change it back.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for sharing this extensive feedback. Please know that this is something we will take into consideration and it has not gone unnoticed. We really appreciate you taking the time to let us know what you think.

reviews.review_description: I bought this game for my child but I wanted to check it out first so I played it for a while.  It was OK, but apparently there is no way to reset your game play back to the beginning.  Not sure how the developers could be so idiotic not to include such  feature.

reviews.review_title: I like it but it’s more of a one time
reviews.review_description: It’s a fun game but it is a game that I only like playing 1 time and then it’s not that fun

Was worried about this game going in since basketball was always the best game.  The new controls haven't ruined it too much.  
 Not the greatest game though.  A bit shallow and still feels like a Gear VR game
One of the very few games my 5 year old can play. She plays for awhile and does great! She gets so excited and loves it so much.
It is a cute game

I had this game for the Google Daydream years ago.  It was perfectly executed and the controls were great.  Same with the Oculus GO.  The Quest version leaves a LOT to be desired.  I think some people will find it cute, but for anyone who expects the physics to actually work - you may be disappointed.
Biggest problem: Basketball.  The balls simply does *NOT* go where I throw it.  I have to see how far off the ball goes, and aim about 2-3 times further off to the side and hope for the best.  Broken in my opinion.  
Next problem: Golf.  Why not use full VR to let the golf club do the aiming??  Instead you point an "arrow" where you want to go and it doesn't matter which way you swing the club.  The ball feels like it's being stopped by quick-sand.  If you try to hit the ball hard enough to go where you need, it simply bounces upward and moves a few feet.  Frustrating. 
I didn't understand the mallet game...you hit some things, they break.  You hit other things (identical items) and they don't break.  Some type of explanation would have been nice. 
The firefighter, cow racing, and wand games worked well, but the other three were my favorites on the non-quest versions.  
This is the first Quest game I'll actually request a refund on.

This carnival-themed collection of six cute and cheerful minigames is both hit and miss, depending on the types of things you enjoy (we particularly enjoyed the firehose game). Very much aimed towards children, you unlock minigames one at a time until you’ve played each one at least once. With random chances to earn extra tokens with each play, which you can redeem and spend on a wheel of fortune, unlocking cosmetics and items you can use with each of the minigames.

You guide your character around a fairground, selecting each minigame and its difficulty variant by walking towards it. There’s maybe some fun to be had as an adult here, but it will be limited to an hour or so worth of play. Luckily the game is fairly priced, so you won’t feel short-changed. This started as an Oculus Go/Gear VR game, and while it plays perfectly well on the Quest, it doesn’t do much else to take advantage of the new 6DoF technology, just allows for more fluid and better-tracked movement. The golf game, however, seems overly wonky and not optimised fully to 6DoF controls. Would nice to see a few games made specifically with the Quest in mind.

Areas for improvement would be firstly multiplayer, right now this is just pass-and-play, which can barely be considered multiplayer. Your character seems to have a stock look, you can’t customise the way they look outside of unlocked cosmetics, which was an odd choice considered there are so many other kids at the carnival they could have allowed you to pick from. It would have been nice to allow you to control your character with the thumbstick on the Quest controllers, as opposed to pointing and clicking where you want him/her to move, this was clearly left over from the Go version and seems an odd choice when playing on the Quest. Besides all that, it’s a nice collection of child-friendly minigames that should keep 'em entertained.

Read the full review and more here: https://www.opiumpulses.com/article/411/10-more-oculus-quest-game-reviews-p2

This could be really fun, but the games are very finicky .e.g. shooting hoops is totally off, you need to almost aim to one side and put way less effort than you'd expect. Golf seems to sometimes flick the ball off in a random direction instead of where you're aiming. Main problem though is that it's listed as multiplayer, but it's not headset vs headset, it's local "pass and play". Not what I was expecting at all.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback. We are taking this to heart and hopefully we can improve these points in the future. If you want to share any more feedback, you are more than welcome to email us at support@resolutiongames.com.

Nice little time waster for younger players, 6 mini games 5 of which are quite fun. Play each game to unlock golden tickets to purchase in-game collectibles for your avatar but after my first impressions video I feel it’s overpriced for the content. £5/$7 would of been more acceptable in my mind. Possible one to check out in a sale ✌️
Little worried when want to buy because read some review have bad controller. But after buy and try it, no problem for me except golf. Its fun mini games for all age and price only $7.99
